business_ideas.idea: Mobile kitchens: restaurants-on-wheels delivering hot food to customers.
Sports stock market: buy/sell players as stocks leveraging new gambling laws.
Conversational commerce: AI-powered personal shopping via text/voice.
Healthcare dashboard: aggregates home diagnostics, AI provides personalized health insights.
Fit analytics: AI predicts apparel fit without trying on, reducing 40% return rates.

predictions.text: Search engines will be obsolete; conversational commerce replaces keyword search.
Home health diagnostics will replace annual doctor visits for most health monitoring.
People will take daily health actions beyond brushing teeth with home devices.

dynamics: Marc drives conversation with expert insights; hosts ask clarifying questions and build on ideas. Interview spans philosophy (hiring, culture, vision) to concrete opportunities. Energy is enthusiastic; hosts act as engaged learners. Structure: opening philosophy, deep hiring/leadership discussion, then rapid-fire idea brainstorm.
frameworks: Vision Capital People (VCP): capital + vision + great people = company success
SPODIC traits: Smart, Passionate, Optimistic, Tenacious, Adaptable, Kind, Empathetic hiring criteria
Resume trajectory analysis: top 5% move in step-fashion with step-changes between roles
Barbell strategy: acquire for talent (small) or go big enough to matter strategically
Trust first model: start trusting people until they prove otherwise
Follow the money: study VC investment trends to spot emerging market opportunities
learnings: Hiring for personal chemistry introduces unconscious bias; resume trajectory is a more objective predictor of performer quality.
The $10M–$100M exit range is hardest: too expensive for aqua-hire, too small to justify strategic bet.
Kindness paired with tenacity prevents toxic high-performers from burning out teams.
Transparency on compensation, cap table, and financials builds trust and ownership culture.
You don't need domain expertise to start a company if you master hiring, capital, and vision communication.
Market tailwinds matter more than novelty; find big existing markets and execute better.

banger_quotes.quote: It's never really about the idea. It's about execution, vision, capital, people.
If the vision is right and you've got great people, great things will happen.
Trust—you trust me, I'm not going to burn you, I'll run through a wall for you.
Optimism allows you to be bold and take risk and believe people are good.

numbers_dropped: Archer seed: $5M → Series A $50M → $3.7B valuation in 2 years
notable: Marc Lore does not own a computer; conducts all business via iPhone, with an assistant reading/relaying emails (ultra-high-net-worth operating model).
